text:
There once was a wise old owl named Oliver who lived high in the ancient forest. His favorite thing to do was think deeply about life's mysteries. One summer dawn, Oliver caught a faint buzzword fluttering through the branches. Following the sound, he stumbled upon a garden full of singing birds.

Curious, he couldn't resist joining them. They chirped melodiously, but one bird sang in a voice as soft as moss. Oliver gently approached her. The bird squawked something like, "Oliver the wise?"

He considered the unusual sound. "Well, it seems some birds can speak," Oliver replied thoughtfully. He listened closely to their conversation.

After a while, they shared stories. The birds fluttered in different directions, visiting every flower and twig, while Oliver listened from a comfy rock.

At last, they tired out. One bird stretched wings out wide like the branches she had come from. Oliver could tell just from its posture things would never be roosted
